behavior_summary: U8P_FEBARSNS::look is a simple 'U8P_FEBARSNS::look' (examine) handler that presents a one-line descriptive bark 'iron bars' when the player examines the associated object or area.
After emitting the bark the process immediately suspends/ends; there is no branching, state mutation, menu interaction, or external calls.
This handler functions purely as an informational UI trigger attached to an in-game object or trigger region (likely a gate, cell, or set of bars).
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: process entry -> emit Bark('iron bars') -> suspend/terminate the U8P_FEBARSNS::look process.
No conditional branches, no flag reads/writes, and no function calls observed.
open_questions: Which concrete in-game object or world location is this script attached to (gate, cell, decorative bars, or fast-area trigger)?
Is this handler invoked by a physical object 'U8P_FEBARSNS::look' action or by entering a fast-area trigger region that also uses the U8P_FEBARSNS::look process?
Does the suspend node correspond to an engine suspend that simply ends the immediate U8P_FEBARSNS::look action, or to a longer-lived suspended process that could be resumed elsewhere?

main_function.name: U8P_FEBARSNS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short on-screen description when the player examines the associated object/area by emitting the text 'iron bars' and then ending the U8P_FEBARSNS::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine's 'U8P_FEBARSNS::look' (examine) action on the linked object or area.
Inferred: may also be triggered by examining an area or by entering a relevant fast-area trigger region associated with the object.

function_map.name: U8P_FEBARSNS::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_FEBARSNS::look handler that displays a descriptive bark and ends the process
